concepts.title: Differentiation of determinant
concepts.conceptBody:
Differential coefficient of a determinant

Let $y=\left| \begin{array}{*{35}{l}} {{f}_{1}}(x) & {{f}_{2}}(x) & {{f}_{3}}(x) \\ {{g}_{1}}(x) & {{g}_{2}}(x) & {{g}_{3}}(x) \\ {{h}_{1}}(x) & {{h}_{2}}(x) & {{h}_{3}}(x) \\ \end{array} \right|$ where ${{f}_{i}}(x),{{g}_{i}}(x),{{h}_{i}}(x),i=1,2,3$ are differentiable functions of $x$. 

\(\frac{d y}{d x}=\left|\begin{array}{lll} f_1^{\prime}(x) & f_2^{\prime}(x) & f_3^{\prime}(x) \\ g_1(x) & g_2(x) & g_3(x) \\ h_1(x) & h_2(x) & h_3(x) \end{array}\right|+\left|\begin{array}{lll} f_1(x) & f_2(x) & f_3(x) \\ g_1^{\prime}(x) & g_2^{\prime}(x) & g_3^{\prime}(x) \\ h_1(x) & h_2(x) & h_3(x) \end{array}\right|\)\(+\left|\begin{array}{lll} f_1(x) & f_2(x) & f_3(x) \\ g_1(x) & g_2(x) & g_3(x) \\ h_1^{\prime}(x) & h_2^{\prime}(x) & h_3^{\prime}(x) \end{array}\right|\)

concepts.title: Integration of determinant
concepts.conceptBody:
Integration of a Determinant

If $f(x)=\left| \begin{matrix} f & g & h \\ a & b & c \\ {{a}_{1}} & {{b}_{1}} & {{c}_{1}} \\ \end{matrix} \right|$

when $\text{f},\text{g},\text{h}$ are the function of $\text{x}$ and $\text{a},\text{b},\text{c},{{\text{a}}_{1}},~{{\text{b}}_{1}},{{\text{c}}_{1}}$ are constants, then

$\int_{\lambda }^{\mu }{F}(x)dx=\left| \begin{matrix} \int_{\lambda }^{\mu }{fdx} & \int_{\lambda }^{\mu }{gdx} & \int_{\lambda }^{\mu }{hdx} \\ a & b & c \\ {{a}_{1}} & ~{{b}_{1}} & {{c}_{1}} \\ \end{matrix} \right|$

Also if $F(x)=\left| \begin{array}{*{35}{l}} f & a & {{a}_{1}} \\ g & b & {{b}_{1}} \\ h & c & {{c}_{1}} \\ \end{array} \right|$

then $\int_{\lambda }^{\mu }{\mathbf{F}}(\text{x})\text{dx}=\left| \begin{array}{*{35}{l}} \int_{\lambda }^{\mu }{\text{fdx}} & \text{a} & {{\text{a}}_{1}} \\ \int_{\lambda }^{\mu }{\text{gdx}} & \text{b} & {{\text{b}}_{1}} \\ \int_{\lambda }^{\mu }{\text{hdx}} & \text{c} & {{\text{c}}_{1}} \\ \end{array} \right|$

examples.title: $\Delta(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}x^n & \sin x & \cos x \\ n! & \sin \frac{n \pi}{2} & \cos \frac{n \pi}{2} \\ a & a^2 & a^3\end{array}\right|$ then the value of $\frac{d^n}{d x^n}[\Delta(x)]$ at $x=0$ is
